mechanism_narrative: A rising trend in nonfarm payrolls indicates expanding household income and aggregate demand, which drives corporate revenue expectations and positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC below 0.03 over a 3-year rolling window or negative mean return when PAYEMS is in its top quartile.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate is pinned below 0.25%, the zero lower bound constraint amplifies the marginal impact of any macro surprise on equity risk premiums, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter as investors reach for yield.
failure_condition: S&P 500 produces negative 60-day returns in more than 50% of months when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25%.

mechanism_narrative: Jointly accelerating inflation and rising unemployment signal stagflationary demand destruction that compresses profit margins and elevates equity risk premiums, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05) during joint 3-month rises in CPIAUCSL and UNRATE.

mechanism_narrative: During late-cycle expansions marked by capacity utilization pressures, a month-over-month decline in industrial production signals an imminent growth slowdown that triggers rapid de-risking and negative S&P 500 returns over the next week.
failure_condition: S&P 500 generates positive 5-day returns in more than 55% of instances when INDPRO declines during periods of low unemployment.

mechanism_narrative: Residential construction starts exceeding one million units annually signal durable credit creation and upstream demand for materials and labor, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over the following quarter.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative or insignificantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.05) when HOUST is above 1000.

mechanism_narrative: The combination of restrictive monetary policy indicated by elevated federal funds rates and decelerating retail sales creates a double drag on corporate earnings multiples and top-line growth, generating negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Rank IC for the interaction term between FEDFUNDS and RSAFS falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of well-anchored inflation expectations, an upside surprise in consumer prices forces a rapid repricing of near-term monetary policy risk, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next week as real discount rates adjust higher.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered high-inflation-print episodes.

mechanism_narrative: A persistent upward trend in retail sales indicates robust consumer spending that translates directly into revenue growth for S&P 500 constituents, producing positive equity returns over the subsequent month.
failure_condition: Rank correlation between RSAFS momentum and 20-day forward returns is below 0.02 over a 3-year rolling window.
